title: fix: strip cache_control from message normalization
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Cache me outside
gist.blurb: Some API providers get confused when you speak Anthropic to them. We stopped sending cache instructions to models that didn't ask for them.
gist.summary: Removed Anthropic-specific `cache_control` parameters from the generic message normalizer to fix compatibility with strict OpenAI-style APIs.
gist.impact: Fixes 400 errors when using Zhipu/GLM-5 and other strict providers that reject unknown fields.
gist.visual_concept: Nomnom creature eating 'cache_control' code blocks that were clogging the pipes. Polly the bee supervising the cleanup.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan nomnom creature happily eating a stack of 'CACHE' code blocks. The cute bee mascot hovers nearby holding a clipboard. Lime green (#ecf874) and lavender soft gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm.
